steps.title: Find the common factor
steps.content:
Both terms in $14w^2+9w$ contain a factor of $w$. Factoring $w$ out of each term gives
$$
14w^2+9w=w(14w+9)
$$

steps.title: Check by distributing
steps.content: Distributing $w$ returns the original expression: $$ w(14w+9)=14w^2+9w $$ Choice B includes an extra factor, choice C changes the degree, and choice D gives coefficients of $23$ and $23$ instead of $14$ and $9$. This keeps exactly the shared factor. The answer is A.
